content.text:
---
title: Split Action
description: Interactive component for split actions.
---

import { SplitActionDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/split-action-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="split-action">
  <SplitActionDemo />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/split-action"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/split-action.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { SplitActions } from "@/components/ui/split-action";

export function MyComponent() {
  const actions = [
    { icon: PenTool, label: "Edit" },
    { icon: Share2, label: "Share" },
  ];
  return (
    <SplitActions
      actions={actions}
      onActionClick={(action, index) => {
        console.log(`Clicked ${action.label} at index ${index}`);
      }}
    />
  );
}
```

## Props

| Prop            | Type                                      | Default | Description                                                                                                     |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `actions`       | `Action[]`                                | -       | Array of action objects with `icon` and `label`                                                                 |
| `triggerIcon`   | `LucideIcon`                              | `Plus`  | Icon for the trigger button                                                                                     |
| `onActionClick` | `(action: Action, index: number) => void` | -       | Callback fired when an action button is clicked. The component closes automatically after an action is clicked. |

### Action

| Property | Type         | Description                       |
| -------- | ------------ | --------------------------------- |
| `icon`   | `LucideIcon` | Icon displayed in the button      |
| `label`  | `string`     | Text label displayed next to icon |

content.text:
---
title: Accordion
description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes.
---

import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/accordion-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="accordion">
  <BouncyAccordionPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";

const items: AccordionItem[] = [
  {
    id: "item-1",
    title: "First question",
    description: "Answer to the first question.",
  },
  {
    id: "item-2",
    title: "Second question",
    description: "Answer to the second question.",
  },
];

export function MyComponent() {
  return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
}
```

## Props

| Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
| --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
| `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
| `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
| `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
| `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
| `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |

### AccordionItem

| Field         | Type        | Description                              |
| ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
| `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
| `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
| `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |

### AccordionClassNames

| Key           | Description                  |
| ------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
| `item`        | Each accordion row container |
| `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
| `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
| `title`       | The title text span          |
| `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
| `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
| `description` | The description text div     |

arguments.content:
---
title: Editable Chip
description: An inline editable chip component with animated transitions between view and edit modes.
---

import { EditableChipDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="editable-chip">
  <EditableChipDemo />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip";

export function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <EditableChip
      defaultLabel="Watchlist"
      onChange={(value) => console.log("New label:", value)}
    />
  );
}
```

## Props

| Prop           | Type                       | Default      | Description                                    |
| -------------- | -------------------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `defaultLabel` | `string`                   | `"Watchlist"` | Initial label text for the chip                |
| `onChange`     | `(value: string) => void`  | -            | Callback fired when the label is saved         |

content.text:
---
title: Editable Chip
description: An inline editable chip component with animated transitions between view and edit modes.
---

import { EditableChipDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="editable-chip">
  <EditableChipDemo />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip";

export function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <EditableChip
      defaultLabel="Watchlist"
      onChange={(value) => console.log("New label:", value)}
    />
  );
}
```

## Props

| Prop           | Type                      | Default       | Description                            |
| -------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `defaultLabel` | `string`                  | `"Watchlist"` | Initial label text for the chip        |
| `onChange`     | `(value: string) => void` | -             | Callback fired when the label is saved |
